event: lying on the floor throughout the night
emergently brought to our Nephrology, Dialysis and Transplantation Unit
no other source of sepsis found
traces of blood in bladder catheter
history of no previous renal impairment
serum creatinine prior to admission: 0.8 mg/dL
use of statins was not recorded
furosemide 125 mg by intravenous infusion daily started
femoral central venous catheter placed (Mahurkar 20 cm, 12 Fr)
endogenous ultrafiltrate flow per session 14 L
low molecular weight heparin (enoxaparin) 4,000 IU bolus on starting dialysis
furosemide tapered to 50 mg per day with oral administration
route of furosemide changed to oral
blood cultures showed growth of E. coli resistant to piperacillin/tazobactam
right jugular central venous catheter placed
dialysis therapy prolonged with on-line hemodiafiltration, 3 sessions
dialysis therapy prolonged with high-flux hemodialysis, 2 sessions
